items.title: Fleet & Corporate Charging Management: operating discovery
items.name: Fleet & Corporate Charging Management: operating discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether fleet & corporate charging management can move the linked customer and business KPIs while addressing session reliability, roaming clarity, and depot-readiness as charger density and utilization rise.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether this bet is strong enough to scale in the next planning cycle, or whether the organization should narrow scope and protect focus.

linkedInitiatives.description: Fleet & Corporate Charging Management Initiative is the quarter's focused investment to move Reduced Fleet TCO and Fleet Contract Value, anchored in session reliability, roaming clarity, and depot-readiness as charger density and utilization rise and led through Fleet Management. The quarter concentrates on charger reliability, roaming transparency, and fleet-depot readiness as networks scale so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.

assignments.how: Leads the discovery framing, evidence synthesis, and recommendation on how far this initiative should scale.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.

assignments.teamName: Driver Identity & Experience - Ev Driver App
assignments.how: Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Customers will engage more consistently if fleet & corporate charging management measurably improves reduced fleet tco.
evidenceToCollect: Whether target users describe reduced fleet tco as a top-priority pain point.
Whether the proposed workflow changes are strong enough to change behavior, not just preference.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The investment is worth scaling when it improves fleet contract value without creating disproportionate operating cost or support load.
evidenceToCollect: Which economic or operating constraints cap the upside.
What minimum KPI movement would justify continued investment.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Users can complete the redesigned workflow with less guidance and less back-and-forth than the current path.
evidenceToCollect: Where users hesitate in the new flow.
Which messages, states, or transitions remain unclear.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can deliver the smallest useful slice inside current architecture and staffing constraints without creating a long tail of one-off work.
evidenceToCollect: What can be standardized versus localized or custom.

plannedActivities: Analyze failed-session cohorts by charger type, roaming path, and market.
Interview roaming-heavy drivers and fleet managers about confidence gaps before session start.
Model whether depot and fleet workflows should be standardized separately from public-network journeys.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether the next investment should optimize the mainstream public-network path or split more aggressively into fleet and roaming operating models.

outcome.summary: Discovery closed enough uncertainty to make a concrete sequencing and investment decision for the following quarter.
